title: Kernel bootstrap lane subagent6. Inspect only the repo-local installer/shim path for making slash commands usable in Codex sessions, especially scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh and adjacent files. Do not edit. Return the minimal patch target, the specific function or command flow involved,...
summary_text: Minimal patch target: [`scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh) in the helper-resolution preflight block (`PROMPTS_ONLY != true`, lines ~152-169), specifically the `resolve_helper_source` command substitutions and `ensure_helper_source` gating. Specific flow involved: 1. `install-kernel-launchers.sh` resolves `codex-prompt-launch` / `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint` first (via `resolve_helper_source`). 2. If they are missing in a clean shell, `set -e` exits before prompt install runs. 3. The prompt install loop (`install_prompt` for `kernel/k/vote/v`, lines ~568-573) never executes, so Codex slash commands are not provisioned. Why this is the smallest fix: 1. It is a single-file, single-flow change. 2. It directly unblocks slash usability (`~/.codex/prompts/*.md`) without changing launcher behavior in [`scripts/local/launchers/kernel`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel), [`v`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/v), or [`vote`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/vote). 3. It avoids broader refactors (new shims, guard logic, or prompt content changes).
